source_pr_title: Draft: add recommendation chatbot evaluation application
source_commit_subject: feat(recommendation-chatbot-eval): RecBot Studio
source_commit_body:
A workbench for testing and evaluating a conversational recommender — RecAI's
in-process InteRecAgent over a native SASRec ranker — with two surfaces:

- Chat: turn-by-turn debugging; per-turn tool-plan / ranked-candidate inspector.
- Persona Eval: an LLM user-simulator drives a full multi-turn conversation
  against the agent across a 336-persona catalog, then scores the result.

A FastAPI backend serves a built React/Vite SPA at a single origin. Item catalogs
ship as small parquet (data/catalogs/) and the persona catalog is bundled in-app
(data/personas/), so a fresh clone can browse and drive the UI with no large
download; scripts/setup_resources.py fetches the ~1.2 GB recommender bundle for
live recommendations. RecAI is a pristine submodule; its tool-plan parser is made
robust to double-encoded plans via a runtime patch in our bridge.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

source_commit_subject: studio: make REAL-mode launch cluster-independent (single .env.local)
source_commit_body:
Remove the author's hardcoded /mira cache paths and absolute venv path from the
committed launch scripts so teammates on other clusters run the app unchanged.
Machine-specific config now lives only in a gitignored .env.local at one
canonical location: the app dir, next to run_demo.sh.

- run_real.sh: drop the /mira HF_HOME / TRANSFORMERS_CACHE /
  SENTENCE_TRANSFORMERS_HOME defaults (caches fall back to ~/.cache).
- requirements.txt: repo-root-relative .venv/bin/python in the install hint.
- run_dev.sh: load .env.local only from the app dir (next to run_demo.sh).
- Add .env.local.example template (OPENAI_API_KEY + optional cache redirect).
- README: point the OpenAI-key step at .env.local.example.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

source_commit_subject: Recategorize new external curation dimensions to existing categories
source_commit_body:
Replace 'External Curation' with semantically appropriate existing categories:

- PANDORA Big5 (6 dims) → Personality (personality trait scoring)
- PersonaChat (1 dim) → Character (persona descriptions)
- HorizonBench (30 dims) → Distributed across:
  • Interaction (8): advice_delivery, apology_style, emotional_support, therapy,
    follow_up, social_engagement, support_technique, self_esteem_rebuilding
  • Preferences (7): content_length, entertainment, tone, writing_style,
    productivity, technology_assistance, facilitation
  • Values (4): conflict_resolution, ethical_review, stakeholder_consultation,
    motivation_strategy
  • Cognition (3): analytical_approach, empirical_evidence, philosophical_engagement
  • Skills (3): creative_collaboration, public_speaking, event_planning
  • Linguistic (3): communication_intimacy, communication_medium, language_preferences
  • Cultural (2): interfaith_engagement, intergenerational_engagement
- WildChat (3 dims) → Demographic (geographic location metadata)

All dimensions maintain full source_origin traceability.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

source_commit_subject: Replace programming language proficiencies with coding style dimensions
source_commit_body:
Refocus Programming category from language proficiency flags to
meaningful coding writing style and philosophy dimensions.

REMOVED (33 dimensions):
  • prog_python, prog_javascript, prog_typescript, prog_java, prog_c, prog_go,
    prog_rust, prog_ruby, prog_php, prog_swift, prog_kotlin, prog_objective_c,
    prog_scala, prog_haskell, prog_elixir, prog_erlang, prog_clojure, prog_lua,
    prog_perl, prog_r, and 13 others
  → Simple language proficiency flags (not discriminating)

ADDED (12 dimensions):
  • code_comment_style: Extensive vs sparse vs self-documenting comments
  • code_summary_documentation: TLDR/summary at file/function beginning ⭐
  • code_naming_verbosity: Verbose vs terse naming conventions
  • code_indentation_style: Tabs vs spaces, indentation width
  • code_structure_preference: Functional vs OOP vs procedural paradigm
  • code_function_length: Small focused vs large multi-purpose functions
  • code_error_handling: Defensive try/catch vs graceful degradation
  • code_abstraction_level: Abstract generic vs concrete problem-specific
  • code_dependencies_approach: Library-heavy vs minimal dependencies
  • code_performance_priority: Performance vs readability trade-off
  • code_testing_approach: TDD vs comprehensive vs minimal testing

source_commit_subject: Add 10 Wikipedia-derived dimensions for real-world persona grounding
source_commit_body:
Integrated biographical dimensions from Wikipedia and Wikidata based on
from_real_people.md dataset recommendations. These dimensions ground
personas in real biographical data from 6M+ Wikipedia articles.

NEW DIMENSIONS (10 total):
1. wiki_birth_date - Birth date (enables age calculation)
2. wiki_birth_place - Geographic birthplace
3. wiki_nationality - Country of citizenship
4. wiki_occupation - Primary profession
5. wiki_field_of_work - Academic/professional discipline
6. wiki_position_held - Official roles (CEO, President, Minister, etc.)
7. wiki_education_level - Educational attainment
8. wiki_awards_recognition - Major prizes and distinctions
9. wiki_marital_status - Marriage/partnership status
10. wiki_political_affiliation - Political party association

DATA COVERAGE:
- High (85-95%): Demographics (birth, nationality, occupation)
- Medium (55-81%): Professional/education (field, positions, awards)
- Low (0%): Derived traits (OCEAN personality - requires inference)

SOURCES:
- michaelauli/wiki_bio (728K pre-extracted biographies)
- rcds/wikipedia-persons-masked (70k Wikipedia people pages)
- wikimedia/structured-wikipedia (Official Wikimedia structured data)
- Wikidata properties (P569, P19, P27, P106, P39, P69, P166, P26, P102)

Total dimensions now: 1388 (from 1378)

These dimensions enable agent simulation grounded in real-world biographical
patterns while maintaining compatibility with existing persona space.

source_commit_subject: Chore: Set up CODEOWNERS, documentation hub, and PR review workflow
source_commit_body:
CODEOWNERS & Team Structure
- Update .github/CODEOWNERS with folder-based code ownership
  • personas/       → @YuexingHao
  • environments/   → @JianhengHou
  • applications/   → @ShirleyHuang11
  • docs/           → @XiaominLi1998
  • * (global)      → @YuexingHao
- CODEOWNERS auto-requests appropriate reviewers for PRs touching each folder
- Enables scalable code review as team grows

Documentation Hub (docs/)
- Add CURRENT_STATE.md: Project snapshot (update weekly)
- Add ROADMAP.md: Long-term vision, milestones, and critical path
- Add TEAM_ASSIGNMENTS.md: Task tracking and responsibility matrix
- Add KNOWN_ISSUES.md: Backlog, blockers, and priority tracking
- Add PR_AND_REVIEW_MANAGEMENT.md: PR workflow guide for all team members
- Add BRANCH_PROTECTION_SETUP.md: Technical guide for setting up protection rules
- Add PR_REVIEW_CHECKLIST.md: Quality standards for PR reviews
- Add README.md: Central navigation hub for documentation
- Add SETUP_COMPLETE.md: Setup summary and status

Deduplication Subdirectory (docs/deduplication/)
- Add IMPLEMENTATION_TRACKING.md: Live status tracker for Phase 1 & 2
- Copy analysis files: FINDINGS_SUMMARY.txt, DEDUP_QUICK_REFERENCE.md,
  DEDUPLICATION_ANALYSIS.txt (for easy access alongside tracker)

Process Documentation (docs/processes/)
- Add MERGE_CONFLICTS.md: How to resolve and prevent merge conflicts
- Add PERSONA_GENERATION.md: Guide for generating and validating personas
- Add SUBMISSION_REQUIREMENTS.md: Contribution standards and checklist

Branch Protection & Auto-Merge Setup
- Add .github/BRANCH_PROTECTION_CHECKLIST.md: Quick step-by-step checklist
  (15 min setup for branch protection on main branch)

Why This Matters
- Clarity: Everyone knows who reviews what (CODEOWNERS)
- Automation: Auto-request saves manual coordination
- Quality: Branch protection enforces review + checks before merge
- Scalability: System works as team grows (just update CODEOWNERS)
- Tracking: Central docs enable better coordination

Next Steps (Manual)
1. Go to GitHub Settings → Branches → Add rule for 'main'
2. Follow .github/BRANCH_PROTECTION_CHECKLIST.md (10 min setup)
3. Test with sample PR (5 min)
4. Announce to team in Discussions (5 min)

See: docs/PR_AND_REVIEW_MANAGEMENT.md for full workflow guide
See: docs/BRANCH_PROTECTION_SETUP.md for technical details

Related: Deduplication phase 1 launch, team scaling, documentation organization

source_commit_subject: Replace programming language proficiencies with coding style dimensions
source_commit_body:
Refocus Programming category from language proficiency flags to
meaningful coding writing style and philosophy dimensions.

REMOVED (33 dimensions):
  • prog_python, prog_javascript, prog_typescript, prog_java, prog_c, prog_go,
    prog_rust, prog_ruby, prog_php, prog_swift, prog_kotlin, prog_objective_c,
    prog_scala, prog_haskell, prog_elixir, prog_erlang, prog_clojure, prog_lua,
    prog_perl, prog_r, and 13 others
  → Simple language proficiency flags (not discriminating)

ADDED (12 dimensions):
  • code_comment_style: Extensive vs sparse vs self-documenting comments
  • code_summary_documentation: TLDR/summary at file/function beginning ⭐
  • code_naming_verbosity: Verbose vs terse naming conventions
  • code_indentation_style: Tabs vs spaces, indentation width
  • code_structure_preference: Functional vs OOP vs procedural paradigm
  • code_function_length: Small focused vs large multi-purpose functions
  • code_error_handling: Defensive try/catch vs graceful degradation
  • code_abstraction_level: Abstract generic vs concrete problem-specific
  • code_dependencies_approach: Library-heavy vs minimal dependencies
  • code_performance_priority: Performance vs readability trade-off
  • code_testing_approach: TDD vs comprehensive vs minimal testing
  • code_refactoring_frequency: Continuous vs on-demand refactoring

These capture real coding philosophy and writing patterns language-agnostically.

Dimension count: 1316 → 1295 (-21 net)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

from_real_people.md dataset recommendations. These dimensions ground
personas in real biographical data from 6M+ Wikipedia articles.

NEW DIMENSIONS (10 total):
1. wiki_birth_date - Birth date (enables age calculation)
2. wiki_birth_place - Geographic birthplace
3. wiki_nationality - Country of citizenship
4. wiki_occupation - Primary profession
5. wiki_field_of_work - Academic/professional discipline
6. wiki_position_held - Official roles (CEO, President, Minister, etc.)
7. wiki_education_level - Educational attainment
8. wiki_awards_recognition - Major prizes and distinctions
9. wiki_marital_status - Marriage/partnership status
10. wiki_political_affiliation - Political party association

DATA COVERAGE:
- High (85-95%): Demographics (birth, nationality, occupation)
- Medium (55-81%): Professional/education (field, positions, awards)
- Low (0%): Derived traits (OCEAN personality - requires inference)

SOURCES:
- michaelauli/wiki_bio (728K pre-extracted biographies)
- rcds/wikipedia-persons-masked (70k Wikipedia people pages)
- wikimedia/structured-wikipedia (Official Wikimedia structured data)
- Wikidata properties (P569, P19, P27, P106, P39, P69, P166, P26, P102)

Total dimensions now: 1388 (from 1378)

These dimensions enable agent simulation grounded in real-world biographical
patterns while maintaining compatibility with existing persona space.

  - 3 genders (Man 48%, Woman 48%, Non-binary 4%)

  - All personas logically coherent (no 3-year-old PhDs, etc.)

Languages:
  - 10 native languages with region-appropriate distribution
  - English proficiency varies by region/persona

Each persona includes:
  - Unique ID (ID0001–ID1000)
  - Full name and title
  - Specific age (not just bracket)
  - Narrative biography (1–2 sentences)
  - 25+ dimensions from matrAIx schema
  - Big Five personality traits (random but consistent)

Files:
  - 1,000 individual YAML files (ID0001.yaml → ID1000.yaml)
  - INDEX.md: Comprehensive directory of all personas with stats

Validation applied:
  ✓ Age vs. education alignment
  ✓ Seniority vs. years of experience
  ✓ Family status realistic for age
  ✓ Education + domain coherence
  ✓ Company size appropriate for seniority
  ✓ Language matches region
  ✓ All combinations logically feasible

Example personas:
  - ID0001: Nikhil Desai, 59, South Asia, Mid, Social Services, Single
  - ID0500: Sarah Chen, 27, East Asia, Manager, Arts & Design, Single

source_commit_subject: Add comprehensive dimension constraint matrix (115 rules)
source_commit_body:
Creates realistic persona filtering rules across 1,339 dimensions and 6,644 unique values:
- Age-based constraints: teens can't be married/widowed, age-experience alignment
- Education constraints: low education incompatible with tech domains/executive roles
- Seniority constraints: experience, skill, and career progression alignment
- Domain expertise: domain-specific education and familiarity requirements
- Socioeconomic alignment: education, seniority, and income consistency
- Personality: psychologically rare Big Five combinations
- Language & proficiency: native speaker and multilingualism consistency
- Industry & skills: domain expertise with field familiarity

Files generated:
- dimension_constraints.json: machine-readable sparse format (115 rules)
- dimension_constraints_incompatibilities.csv: index-based matrix lookup
- dimension_constraints_readable.txt: human-readable rule list

Constraint matrix sparsity: 99.9997% (115 rules out of 44.1M possible combinations)

source_commit_subject: Replace programming language proficiencies with coding style dimensions
source_commit_body:
Refocus Programming category from language proficiency flags to
meaningful coding writing style and philosophy dimensions.

REMOVED (33 dimensions):
  • prog_python, prog_javascript, prog_typescript, prog_java, prog_c, prog_go,
    prog_rust, prog_ruby, prog_php, prog_swift, prog_kotlin, prog_objective_c,

    prog_perl, prog_r, and 13 others
  → Simple language proficiency flags (not discriminating)

ADDED (12 dimensions):
  • code_comment_style: Extensive vs sparse vs self-documenting comments
  • code_summary_documentation: TLDR/summary at file/function beginning ⭐
  • code_naming_verbosity: Verbose vs terse naming conventions
  • code_indentation_style: Tabs vs spaces, indentation width
  • code_structure_preference: Functional vs OOP vs procedural paradigm
  • code_function_length: Small focused vs large multi-purpose functions
  • code_error_handling: Defensive try/catch vs graceful degradation
  • code_abstraction_level: Abstract generic vs concrete problem-specific
  • code_dependencies_approach: Library-heavy vs minimal dependencies
  • code_performance_priority: Performance vs readability trade-off
  • code_testing_approach: TDD vs comprehensive vs minimal testing
  • code_refactoring_frequency: Continuous vs on-demand refactoring

These capture real coding philosophy and writing patterns language-agnostically.

Dimension count: 1316 → 1295 (-21 net)

source_commit_subject: Draft: add recommendation chatbot evaluation application (#41)
source_commit_body:
* feat(recommendation-chatbot-eval): RecBot Studio

A workbench for testing and evaluating a conversational recommender — RecAI's
in-process InteRecAgent over a native SASRec ranker — with two surfaces:

- Chat: turn-by-turn debugging; per-turn tool-plan / ranked-candidate inspector.
- Persona Eval: an LLM user-simulator drives a full multi-turn conversation
  against the agent across a 336-persona catalog, then scores the result.

A FastAPI backend serves a built React/Vite SPA at a single origin. Item catalogs
ship as small parquet (data/catalogs/) and the persona catalog is bundled in-app
(data/personas/), so a fresh clone can browse and drive the UI with no large
download; scripts/setup_resources.py fetches the ~1.2 GB recommender bundle for
live recommendations. RecAI is a pristine submodule; its tool-plan parser is made
robust to double-encoded plans via a runtime patch in our bridge.


* studio: make REAL-mode launch cluster-independent (single .env.local)

Remove the author's hardcoded /mira cache paths and absolute venv path from the
committed launch scripts so teammates on other clusters run the app unchanged.
Machine-specific config now lives only in a gitignored .env.local at one
canonical location: the app dir, next to run_demo.sh.

- run_real.sh: drop the /mira HF_HOME / TRANSFORMERS_CACHE /
  SENTENCE_TRANSFORMERS_HOME defaults (caches fall back to ~/.cache).
- requirements.txt: repo-root-relative .venv/bin/python in the install hint.
- run_dev.sh: load .env.local only from the app dir (next to run_demo.sh).
- Add .env.local.example template (OPENAI_API_KEY + optional cache redirect).
- README: point the OpenAI-key step at .env.local.example.

---------

Co-authored-by: Qianfeng Wen <qianfeng.wen@mail.utoronto.ca>

meaningful coding writing style and philosophy dimensions.

  • code_comment_style: Extensive vs sparse vs self-documenting comments
  • code_summary_documentation: TLDR/summary at file/function beginning ⭐
  • code_naming_verbosity: Verbose vs terse naming conventions
  • code_indentation_style: Tabs vs spaces, indentation width
  • code_structure_preference: Functional vs OOP vs procedural paradigm
  • code_function_length: Small focused vs large multi-purpose functions
  • code_error_handling: Defensive try/catch vs graceful degradation
  • code_abstraction_level: Abstract generic vs concrete problem-specific
  • code_dependencies_approach: Library-heavy vs minimal dependencies
  • code_performance_priority: Performance vs readability trade-off
  • code_testing_approach: TDD vs comprehensive vs minimal testing
  • code_refactoring_frequency: Continuous vs on-demand refactoring

These capture real coding philosophy and writing patterns language-agnostically.

Dimension count: 1316 → 1295 (-21 net)

source_commit_subject: Add comprehensive dimension constraint matrix (115 rules)

Creates realistic persona filtering rules across 1,339 dimensions and 6,644 unique values:
- Age-based constraints: teens can't be married/widowed, age-experience alignment
- Education constraints: low education incompatible with tech domains/executive roles
- Seniority constraints: experience, skill, and career progression alignment
- Domain expertise: domain-specific education and familiarity requirements
- Socioeconomic alignment: education, seniority, and income consistency
- Personality: psychologically rare Big Five combinations
- Language & proficiency: native speaker and multilingualism consistency
- Industry & skills: domain expertise with field familiarity

Files generated:
- dimension_constraints.json: machine-readable sparse format (115 rules)
- dimension_constraints_incompatibilities.csv: index-based matrix lookup
- dimension_constraints_readable.txt: human-readable rule list

Constraint matrix sparsity: 99.9997% (115 rules out of 44.1M possible combinations)

source_commit_body:
* feat(recommendation-chatbot-eval): RecBot Studio

A workbench for testing and evaluating a conversational recommender — RecAI's
in-process InteRecAgent over a native SASRec ranker — with two surfaces:

- Chat: turn-by-turn debugging; per-turn tool-plan / ranked-candidate inspector.
- Persona Eval: an LLM user-simulator drives a full multi-turn conversation
  against the agent across a 336-persona catalog, then scores the result.

A FastAPI backend serves a built React/Vite SPA at a single origin. Item catalogs
ship as small parquet (data/catalogs/) and the persona catalog is bundled in-app
(data/personas/), so a fresh clone can browse and drive the UI with no large
download; scripts/setup_resources.py fetches the ~1.2 GB recommender bundle for
live recommendations. RecAI is a pristine submodule; its tool-plan parser is made
robust to double-encoded plans via a runtime patch in our bridge.


* studio: make REAL-mode launch cluster-independent (single .env.local)

Remove the author's hardcoded /mira cache paths and absolute venv path from the
committed launch scripts so teammates on other clusters run the app unchanged.
Machine-specific config now lives only in a gitignored .env.local at one
canonical location: the app dir, next to run_demo.sh.

- run_real.sh: drop the /mira HF_HOME / TRANSFORMERS_CACHE /
  SENTENCE_TRANSFORMERS_HOME defaults (caches fall back to ~/.cache).
- requirements.txt: repo-root-relative .venv/bin/python in the install hint.
- run_dev.sh: load .env.local only from the app dir (next to run_demo.sh).
- Add .env.local.example template (OPENAI_API_KEY + optional cache redirect).
- README: point the OpenAI-key step at .env.local.example.

---------

Co-authored-by: Qianfeng Wen <qianfeng.wen@mail.utoronto.ca>

source_commit_subject: Replace programming language proficiencies with coding style dimensions
source_commit_body:
Refocus Programming category from language proficiency flags to
meaningful coding writing style and philosophy dimensions.

  • prog_python, prog_javascript, prog_typescript, prog_java, prog_c, prog_go,
    prog_rust, prog_ruby, prog_php, prog_swift, prog_kotlin, prog_objective_c,

    prog_perl, prog_r, and 13 others
  → Simple language proficiency flags (not discriminating)

ADDED (12 dimensions):
  • code_comment_style: Extensive vs sparse vs self-documenting comments
  • code_summary_documentation: TLDR/summary at file/function beginning ⭐
  • code_naming_verbosity: Verbose vs terse naming conventions
  • code_indentation_style: Tabs vs spaces, indentation width
  • code_structure_preference: Functional vs OOP vs procedural paradigm
  • code_function_length: Small focused vs large multi-purpose functions
  • code_error_handling: Defensive try/catch vs graceful degradation
  • code_abstraction_level: Abstract generic vs concrete problem-specific
  • code_dependencies_approach: Library-heavy vs minimal dependencies
  • code_performance_priority: Performance vs readability trade-off
  • code_testing_approach: TDD vs comprehensive vs minimal testing
  • code_refactoring_frequency: Continuous vs on-demand refactoring

These capture real coding philosophy and writing patterns language-agnostically.

Dimension count: 1316 → 1295 (-21 net)

source_commit_body:
* feat(recommendation-chatbot-eval): RecBot Studio

A workbench for testing and evaluating a conversational recommender — RecAI's
in-process InteRecAgent over a native SASRec ranker — with two surfaces:

- Chat: turn-by-turn debugging; per-turn tool-plan / ranked-candidate inspector.
- Persona Eval: an LLM user-simulator drives a full multi-turn conversation
  against the agent across a 336-persona catalog, then scores the result.

A FastAPI backend serves a built React/Vite SPA at a single origin. Item catalogs
ship as small parquet (data/catalogs/) and the persona catalog is bundled in-app
(data/personas/), so a fresh clone can browse and drive the UI with no large
download; scripts/setup_resources.py fetches the ~1.2 GB recommender bundle for
live recommendations. RecAI is a pristine submodule; its tool-plan parser is made
robust to double-encoded plans via a runtime patch in our bridge.


* studio: make REAL-mode launch cluster-independent (single .env.local)

Remove the author's hardcoded /mira cache paths and absolute venv path from the
committed launch scripts so teammates on other clusters run the app unchanged.
Machine-specific config now lives only in a gitignored .env.local at one
canonical location: the app dir, next to run_demo.sh.

- run_real.sh: drop the /mira HF_HOME / TRANSFORMERS_CACHE /
  SENTENCE_TRANSFORMERS_HOME defaults (caches fall back to ~/.cache).
- requirements.txt: repo-root-relative .venv/bin/python in the install hint.
- run_dev.sh: load .env.local only from the app dir (next to run_demo.sh).
- Add .env.local.example template (OPENAI_API_KEY + optional cache redirect).
- README: point the OpenAI-key step at .env.local.example.

---------

Co-authored-by: Qianfeng Wen <qianfeng.wen@mail.utoronto.ca>

  • code_indentation_style: Tabs vs spaces, indentation width
  • code_structure_preference: Functional vs OOP vs procedural paradigm
  • code_function_length: Small focused vs large multi-purpose functions
  • code_error_handling: Defensive try/catch vs graceful degradation
  • code_abstraction_level: Abstract generic vs concrete problem-specific
  • code_dependencies_approach: Library-heavy vs minimal dependencies
  • code_performance_priority: Performance vs readability trade-off
  • code_testing_approach: TDD vs comprehensive vs minimal testing
  • code_refactoring_frequency: Continuous vs on-demand refactoring

These capture real coding philosophy and writing patterns language-agnostically.

Dimension count: 1316 → 1295 (-21 net)

Draft: add recommendation chatbot evaluation application (#41)
source_commit_body:
* feat(recommendation-chatbot-eval): RecBot Studio

A workbench for testing and evaluating a conversational recommender — RecAI's
in-process InteRecAgent over a native SASRec ranker — with two surfaces:

- Chat: turn-by-turn debugging; per-turn tool-plan / ranked-candidate inspector.
- Persona Eval: an LLM user-simulator drives a full multi-turn conversation
  against the agent across a 336-persona catalog, then scores the result.

A FastAPI backend serves a built React/Vite SPA at a single origin. Item catalogs
ship as small parquet (data/catalogs/) and the persona catalog is bundled in-app
(data/personas/), so a fresh clone can browse and drive the UI with no large
download; scripts/setup_resources.py fetches the ~1.2 GB recommender bundle for
live recommendations. RecAI is a pristine submodule; its tool-plan parser is made
robust to double-encoded plans via a runtime patch in our bridge.


* studio: make REAL-mode launch cluster-independent (single .env.local)

Remove the author's hardcoded /mira cache paths and absolute venv path from the
committed launch scripts so teammates on other clusters run the app unchanged.
Machine-specific config now lives only in a gitignored .env.local at one
canonical location: the app dir, next to run_demo.sh.

- run_real.sh: drop the /mira HF_HOME / TRANSFORMERS_CACHE /
  SENTENCE_TRANSFORMERS_HOME defaults (caches fall back to ~/.cache).
- requirements.txt: repo-root-relative .venv/bin/python in the install hint.
- run_dev.sh: load .env.local only from the app dir (next to run_demo.sh).
- Add .env.local.example template (OPENAI_API_KEY + optional cache redirect).
- README: point the OpenAI-key step at .env.local.example.

---------

Co-authored-by: Qianfeng Wen <qianfeng.wen@mail.utoronto.ca>

source_commit_subject: Replace programming language proficiencies with coding style dimensions
source_commit_body:
Refocus Programming category from language proficiency flags to
meaningful coding writing style and philosophy dimensions.

REMOVED (33 dimensions):
  • prog_python, prog_javascript, prog_typescript, prog_java, prog_c, prog_go,
    prog_rust, prog_ruby, prog_php, prog_swift, prog_kotlin, prog_objective_c,
    prog_scala, prog_haskell, prog_elixir, prog_erlang, prog_clojure, prog_lua,
    prog_perl, prog_r, and 13 others
  → Simple language proficiency flags (not discriminating)

ADDED (12 dimensions):
  • code_comment_style: Extensive vs sparse vs self-documenting comments
  • code_summary_documentation: TLDR/summary at file/function beginning ⭐
  • code_naming_verbosity: Verbose vs terse naming conventions
  • code_indentation_style: Tabs vs spaces, indentation width
  • code_structure_preference: Functional vs OOP vs procedural paradigm
  • code_function_length: Small focused vs large multi-purpose functions
  • code_error_handling: Defensive try/catch vs graceful degradation
  • code_abstraction_level: Abstract generic vs concrete problem-specific
  • code_dependencies_approach: Library-heavy vs minimal dependencies
  • code_performance_priority: Performance vs readability trade-off
  • code_testing_approach: TDD vs comprehensive vs minimal testing
  • code_refactoring_frequency: Continuous vs on-demand refactoring

These capture real coding philosophy and writing patterns language-agnostically.

Dimension count: 1316 → 1295 (-21 net)

- dimension_constraints_incompatibilities.csv: index-based matrix lookup
- dimension_constraints_readable.txt: human-readable rule list

source_commit_body:
* feat(recommendation-chatbot-eval): RecBot Studio

A workbench for testing and evaluating a conversational recommender — RecAI's
in-process InteRecAgent over a native SASRec ranker — with two surfaces:

- Chat: turn-by-turn debugging; per-turn tool-plan / ranked-candidate inspector.
- Persona Eval: an LLM user-simulator drives a full multi-turn conversation
  against the agent across a 336-persona catalog, then scores the result.

A FastAPI backend serves a built React/Vite SPA at a single origin. Item catalogs
ship as small parquet (data/catalogs/) and the persona catalog is bundled in-app
(data/personas/), so a fresh clone can browse and drive the UI with no large
download; scripts/setup_resources.py fetches the ~1.2 GB recommender bundle for
live recommendations. RecAI is a pristine submodule; its tool-plan parser is made
robust to double-encoded plans via a runtime patch in our bridge.


* studio: make REAL-mode launch cluster-independent (single .env.local)

Remove the author's hardcoded /mira cache paths and absolute venv path from the
committed launch scripts so teammates on other clusters run the app unchanged.
Machine-specific config now lives only in a gitignored .env.local at one
canonical location: the app dir, next to run_demo.sh.

- run_real.sh: drop the /mira HF_HOME / TRANSFORMERS_CACHE /
  SENTENCE_TRANSFORMERS_HOME defaults (caches fall back to ~/.cache).
- requirements.txt: repo-root-relative .venv/bin/python in the install hint.
- run_dev.sh: load .env.local only from the app dir (next to run_demo.sh).
- Add .env.local.example template (OPENAI_API_KEY + optional cache redirect).
- README: point the OpenAI-key step at .env.local.example.

---------

Co-authored-by: Qianfeng Wen <qianfeng.wen@mail.utoronto.ca>

source_commit_subject: Replace programming language proficiencies with coding style dimensions
source_commit_body:
Refocus Programming category from language proficiency flags to
meaningful coding writing style and philosophy dimensions.

    prog_rust, prog_ruby, prog_php, prog_swift, prog_kotlin, prog_objective_c,

ADDED (12 dimensions):
  • code_comment_style: Extensive vs sparse vs self-documenting comments
  • code_summary_documentation: TLDR/summary at file/function beginning ⭐
  • code_naming_verbosity: Verbose vs terse naming conventions
  • code_indentation_style: Tabs vs spaces, indentation width
  • code_structure_preference: Functional vs OOP vs procedural paradigm
  • code_function_length: Small focused vs large multi-purpose functions
  • code_error_handling: Defensive try/catch vs graceful degradation
  • code_abstraction_level: Abstract generic vs concrete problem-specific
  • code_dependencies_approach: Library-heavy vs minimal dependencies
  • code_performance_priority: Performance vs readability trade-off
  • code_testing_approach: TDD vs comprehensive vs minimal testing
  • code_refactoring_frequency: Continuous vs on-demand refactoring

These capture real coding philosophy and writing patterns language-agnostically.

Dimension count: 1316 → 1295 (-21 net)

source_commit_subject: Generate 1,000 diverse, validated personas (ID0001–ID1000)
source_commit_body:
Created 1,000 realistic, human-coherent personas covering:

Geographic Diversity:
  - 9 regions: East Asia, South Asia, Western Europe, Eastern Europe,
    North America, Latin America, Sub-Saharan Africa, MENA, Oceania
  - 100–115 personas per region

Seniority & Career:
  - 8 levels: Student/intern, Entry, Mid, Senior, Manager, Founder/CEO,
    C-suite, Lead
  - Realistic experience progression (0–15+ years)
  - 12 professional domains

Demographics:
  - Ages 13–75 with realistic age/education/family coherence
  - 3 genders (Man 48%, Woman 48%, Non-binary 4%)
  - 5 socioeconomic bands
  - 4 urbanicity levels

Education & Family:
  - All education levels (high school through PhD)
  - Family status realistic for age (single→married→adult children)
  - All personas logically coherent (no 3-year-old PhDs, etc.)

Languages:
  - 10 native languages with region-appropriate distribution
  - English proficiency varies by region/persona

Each persona includes:
  - Unique ID (ID0001–ID1000)
  - Full name and title
  - Specific age (not just bracket)
  - Narrative biography (1–2 sentences)
  - 25+ dimensions from matrAIx schema
  - Big Five personality traits (random but consistent)

Files:
  - 1,000 individual YAML files (ID0001.yaml → ID1000.yaml)
  - INDEX.md: Comprehensive directory of all personas with stats

Validation applied:
  ✓ Age vs. education alignment
  ✓ Seniority vs. years of experience
  ✓ Family status realistic for age
  ✓ Education + domain coherence
  ✓ Company size appropriate for seniority
  ✓ Language matches region
  ✓ All combinations logically feasible

Example personas:

- Age-based constraints: teens can't be married/widowed, age-experience alignment

- Seniority constraints: experience, skill, and career progression alignment

- Socioeconomic alignment: education, seniority, and income consistency
- Personality: psychologically rare Big Five combinations
- Language & proficiency: native speaker and multilingualism consistency
- Industry & skills: domain expertise with field familiarity

Files generated:

- dimension_constraints_incompatibilities.csv: index-based matrix lookup
- dimension_constraints_readable.txt: human-readable rule list

source_commit_body:
* feat(recommendation-chatbot-eval): RecBot Studio

A workbench for testing and evaluating a conversational recommender — RecAI's
in-process InteRecAgent over a native SASRec ranker — with two surfaces:

- Chat: turn-by-turn debugging; per-turn tool-plan / ranked-candidate inspector.
- Persona Eval: an LLM user-simulator drives a full multi-turn conversation
  against the agent across a 336-persona catalog, then scores the result.

A FastAPI backend serves a built React/Vite SPA at a single origin. Item catalogs
ship as small parquet (data/catalogs/) and the persona catalog is bundled in-app
(data/personas/), so a fresh clone can browse and drive the UI with no large
download; scripts/setup_resources.py fetches the ~1.2 GB recommender bundle for
live recommendations. RecAI is a pristine submodule; its tool-plan parser is made
robust to double-encoded plans via a runtime patch in our bridge.


* studio: make REAL-mode launch cluster-independent (single .env.local)

Remove the author's hardcoded /mira cache paths and absolute venv path from the
committed launch scripts so teammates on other clusters run the app unchanged.
Machine-specific config now lives only in a gitignored .env.local at one
canonical location: the app dir, next to run_demo.sh.

- run_real.sh: drop the /mira HF_HOME / TRANSFORMERS_CACHE /
  SENTENCE_TRANSFORMERS_HOME defaults (caches fall back to ~/.cache).
- requirements.txt: repo-root-relative .venv/bin/python in the install hint.
- run_dev.sh: load .env.local only from the app dir (next to run_demo.sh).
- Add .env.local.example template (OPENAI_API_KEY + optional cache redirect).
- README: point the OpenAI-key step at .env.local.example.

---------

Co-authored-by: Qianfeng Wen <qianfeng.wen@mail.utoronto.ca>

source_commit_subject: Replace programming language proficiencies with coding style dimensions
source_commit_body:
Refocus Programming category from language proficiency flags to
meaningful coding writing style and philosophy dimensions.

REMOVED (33 dimensions):
  • prog_python, prog_javascript, prog_typescript, prog_java, prog_c, prog_go,
    prog_rust, prog_ruby, prog_php, prog_swift, prog_kotlin, prog_objective_c,
    prog_scala, prog_haskell, prog_elixir, prog_erlang, prog_clojure, prog_lua,
    prog_perl, prog_r, and 13 others
  → Simple language proficiency flags (not discriminating)

ADDED (12 dimensions):
  • code_comment_style: Extensive vs sparse vs self-documenting comments
  • code_summary_documentation: TLDR/summary at file/function beginning ⭐
  • code_naming_verbosity: Verbose vs terse naming conventions
  • code_indentation_style: Tabs vs spaces, indentation width
  • code_structure_preference: Functional vs OOP vs procedural paradigm
  • code_function_length: Small focused vs large multi-purpose functions
  • code_error_handling: Defensive try/catch vs graceful degradation
  • code_abstraction_level: Abstract generic vs concrete problem-specific
  • code_dependencies_approach: Library-heavy vs minimal dependencies
  • code_performance_priority: Performance vs readability trade-off
  • code_testing_approach: TDD vs comprehensive vs minimal testing
  • code_refactoring_frequency: Continuous vs on-demand refactoring

DATA COVERAGE:
- High (85-95%): Demographics (birth, nationality, occupation)
- Medium (55-81%): Professional/education (field, positions, awards)
- Low (0%): Derived traits (OCEAN personality - requires inference)

SOURCES:
- michaelauli/wiki_bio (728K pre-extracted biographies)
- rcds/wikipedia-persons-masked (70k Wikipedia people pages)
- wikimedia/structured-wikipedia (Official Wikimedia structured data)
- Wikidata properties (P569, P19, P27, P106, P39, P69, P166, P26, P102)

Documentation Hub (docs/)

Branch Protection & Auto-Merge Setup

  (15 min setup for branch protection on main branch)

Why This Matters
- Clarity: Everyone knows who reviews what (CODEOWNERS)
- Automation: Auto-request saves manual coordination
- Quality: Branch protection enforces review + checks before merge
- Scalability: System works as team grows (just update CODEOWNERS)

2. Follow .github/BRANCH_PROTECTION_CHECKLIST.md (10 min setup)
3. Test with sample PR (5 min)

source_commit_body:
Add advanced scaling script to convert full Hugging Face datasets to YAML format:

Scale-up script (scale_up_datasets.py):
- Handles streaming from Hugging Face Hub (no large downloads needed)
- Supports multiple datasets: Nemotron (1M), PersonaHub, SyntheticPersonaChat
- Memory-efficient streaming write to disk
- Progress tracking with tqdm
- Detailed logging to scale_up.log
- Extensible for future datasets

Nemotron scaling (1M personas):
- 11 parquet shards from nvidia/Nemotron-Personas-USA
- Streaming ensures constant memory usage
- Estimated ~24-48 hours for full dataset

source_commit_subject: Add comprehensive dimension constraint matrix (115 rules)
source_commit_body:
Creates realistic persona filtering rules across 1,339 dimensions and 6,644 unique values:
- Age-based constraints: teens can't be married/widowed, age-experience alignment
- Education constraints: low education incompatible with tech domains/executive roles
- Seniority constraints: experience, skill, and career progression alignment
- Domain expertise: domain-specific education and familiarity requirements
- Socioeconomic alignment: education, seniority, and income consistency
- Personality: psychologically rare Big Five combinations
- Language & proficiency: native speaker and multilingualism consistency
- Industry & skills: domain expertise with field familiarity

Files generated:
- dimension_constraints.json: machine-readable sparse format (115 rules)
- dimension_constraints_incompatibilities.csv: index-based matrix lookup
- dimension_constraints_readable.txt: human-readable rule list

Constraint matrix sparsity: 99.9997% (115 rules out of 44.1M possible combinations)

source_commit_subject: Draft: add recommendation chatbot evaluation application (#41)
source_commit_body:
* feat(recommendation-chatbot-eval): RecBot Studio

A workbench for testing and evaluating a conversational recommender — RecAI's
in-process InteRecAgent over a native SASRec ranker — with two surfaces:

- Chat: turn-by-turn debugging; per-turn tool-plan / ranked-candidate inspector.
- Persona Eval: an LLM user-simulator drives a full multi-turn conversation
  against the agent across a 336-persona catalog, then scores the result.

A FastAPI backend serves a built React/Vite SPA at a single origin. Item catalogs
ship as small parquet (data/catalogs/) and the persona catalog is bundled in-app
(data/personas/), so a fresh clone can browse and drive the UI with no large
download; scripts/setup_resources.py fetches the ~1.2 GB recommender bundle for
live recommendations. RecAI is a pristine submodule; its tool-plan parser is made
robust to double-encoded plans via a runtime patch in our bridge.


* studio: make REAL-mode launch cluster-independent (single .env.local)

Remove the author's hardcoded /mira cache paths and absolute venv path from the
committed launch scripts so teammates on other clusters run the app unchanged.
Machine-specific config now lives only in a gitignored .env.local at one
canonical location: the app dir, next to run_demo.sh.

- run_real.sh: drop the /mira HF_HOME / TRANSFORMERS_CACHE /
  SENTENCE_TRANSFORMERS_HOME defaults (caches fall back to ~/.cache).
- requirements.txt: repo-root-relative .venv/bin/python in the install hint.
- run_dev.sh: load .env.local only from the app dir (next to run_demo.sh).
- Add .env.local.example template (OPENAI_API_KEY + optional cache redirect).
- README: point the OpenAI-key step at .env.local.example.

---------

Co-authored-by: Qianfeng Wen <qianfeng.wen@mail.utoronto.ca>

source_commit_subject: Replace programming language proficiencies with coding style dimensions
source_commit_body:
Refocus Programming category from language proficiency flags to
meaningful coding writing style and philosophy dimensions.

REMOVED (33 dimensions):
  • prog_python, prog_javascript, prog_typescript, prog_java, prog_c, prog_go,
    prog_rust, prog_ruby, prog_php, prog_swift, prog_kotlin, prog_objective_c,
    prog_scala, prog_haskell, prog_elixir, prog_erlang, prog_clojure, prog_lua,
    prog_perl, prog_r, and 13 others
  → Simple language proficiency flags (not discriminating)

ADDED (12 dimensions):
  • code_comment_style: Extensive vs sparse vs self-documenting comments
  • code_summary_documentation: TLDR/summary at file/function beginning ⭐
  • code_naming_verbosity: Verbose vs terse naming conventions
  • code_indentation_style: Tabs vs spaces, indentation width
  • code_structure_preference: Functional vs OOP vs procedural paradigm
  • code_function_length: Small focused vs large multi-purpose functions
  • code_error_handling: Defensive try/catch vs graceful degradation
  • code_abstraction_level: Abstract generic vs concrete problem-specific
  • code_dependencies_approach: Library-heavy vs minimal dependencies
  • code_performance_priority: Performance vs readability trade-off
  • code_testing_approach: TDD vs comprehensive vs minimal testing
  • code_refactoring_frequency: Continuous vs on-demand refactoring

These capture real coding philosophy and writing patterns language-agnostically.

Dimension count: 1316 → 1295 (-21 net)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

VALUES & WORLDVIEW (3): Values & Motivation, Worldview, Risk

BEHAVIOR (4): Habits, Work, Time, Preferences

source_commit_subject: Add 10 Wikipedia-derived dimensions for real-world persona grounding
source_commit_body:
Integrated biographical dimensions from Wikipedia and Wikidata based on
from_real_people.md dataset recommendations. These dimensions ground
personas in real biographical data from 6M+ Wikipedia articles.

NEW DIMENSIONS (10 total):
1. wiki_birth_date - Birth date (enables age calculation)
2. wiki_birth_place - Geographic birthplace
3. wiki_nationality - Country of citizenship
4. wiki_occupation - Primary profession
5. wiki_field_of_work - Academic/professional discipline
6. wiki_position_held - Official roles (CEO, President, Minister, etc.)
7. wiki_education_level - Educational attainment
8. wiki_awards_recognition - Major prizes and distinctions
9. wiki_marital_status - Marriage/partnership status
10. wiki_political_affiliation - Political party association

DATA COVERAGE:
- High (85-95%): Demographics (birth, nationality, occupation)
- Medium (55-81%): Professional/education (field, positions, awards)
- Low (0%): Derived traits (OCEAN personality - requires inference)

SOURCES:
- michaelauli/wiki_bio (728K pre-extracted biographies)
- rcds/wikipedia-persons-masked (70k Wikipedia people pages)
- wikimedia/structured-wikipedia (Official Wikimedia structured data)
- Wikidata properties (P569, P19, P27, P106, P39, P69, P166, P26, P102)

Total dimensions now: 1388 (from 1378)

These dimensions enable agent simulation grounded in real-world biographical
patterns while maintaining compatibility with existing persona space.

source_commit_subject: Chore: Set up CODEOWNERS, documentation hub, and PR review workflow
source_commit_body:
CODEOWNERS & Team Structure
- Update .github/CODEOWNERS with folder-based code ownership
  • personas/       → @YuexingHao
  • environments/   → @JianhengHou
  • applications/   → @ShirleyHuang11
  • docs/           → @XiaominLi1998
  • * (global)      → @YuexingHao
- CODEOWNERS auto-requests appropriate reviewers for PRs touching each folder
- Enables scalable code review as team grows

Documentation Hub (docs/)
- Add CURRENT_STATE.md: Project snapshot (update weekly)
- Add ROADMAP.md: Long-term vision, milestones, and critical path
- Add TEAM_ASSIGNMENTS.md: Task tracking and responsibility matrix
- Add KNOWN_ISSUES.md: Backlog, blockers, and priority tracking
- Add PR_AND_REVIEW_MANAGEMENT.md: PR workflow guide for all team members
- Add BRANCH_PROTECTION_SETUP.md: Technical guide for setting up protection rules
- Add PR_REVIEW_CHECKLIST.md: Quality standards for PR reviews
- Add README.md: Central navigation hub for documentation
- Add SETUP_COMPLETE.md: Setup summary and status

Deduplication Subdirectory (docs/deduplication/)
- Add IMPLEMENTATION_TRACKING.md: Live status tracker for Phase 1 & 2
- Copy analysis files: FINDINGS_SUMMARY.txt, DEDUP_QUICK_REFERENCE.md,
  DEDUPLICATION_ANALYSIS.txt (for easy access alongside tracker)

Process Documentation (docs/processes/)
- Add MERGE_CONFLICTS.md: How to resolve and prevent merge conflicts
- Add PERSONA_GENERATION.md: Guide for generating and validating personas
- Add SUBMISSION_REQUIREMENTS.md: Contribution standards and checklist

Branch Protection & Auto-Merge Setup
- Add .github/BRANCH_PROTECTION_CHECKLIST.md: Quick step-by-step checklist
  (15 min setup for branch protection on main branch)

Why This Matters
- Clarity: Everyone knows who reviews what (CODEOWNERS)
- Automation: Auto-request saves manual coordination
- Quality: Branch protection enforces review + checks before merge
- Scalability: System works as team grows (just update CODEOWNERS)
- Tracking: Central docs enable better coordination

Next Steps (Manual)
1. Go to GitHub Settings → Branches → Add rule for 'main'
2. Follow .github/BRANCH_PROTECTION_CHECKLIST.md (10 min setup)
3. Test with sample PR (5 min)
4. Announce to team in Discussions (5 min)

See: docs/PR_AND_REVIEW_MANAGEMENT.md for full workflow guide
See: docs/BRANCH_PROTECTION_SETUP.md for technical details

Related: Deduplication phase 1 launch, team scaling, documentation organization

source_commit_subject: Feat: Add full dataset scale-up infrastructure for 1M+ personas
source_commit_body:
Add advanced scaling script to convert full Hugging Face datasets to YAML format:

Scale-up script (scale_up_datasets.py):
- Handles streaming from Hugging Face Hub (no large downloads needed)
- Supports multiple datasets: Nemotron (1M), PersonaHub, SyntheticPersonaChat
- Memory-efficient streaming write to disk
- Progress tracking with tqdm
- Detailed logging to scale_up.log
- Extensible for future datasets

Nemotron scaling (1M personas):
- 11 parquet shards from nvidia/Nemotron-Personas-USA
- Streaming ensures constant memory usage
- Estimated ~24-48 hours for full dataset
- Each persona → Nemotron_XXXXXXXX.yaml (UUID-based ID)

PersonaHub scaling (300GB+ dataset):
- Elite persona subset from proj-persona/PersonaHub
- Structured domain expertise extraction
- Streaming mode handles large datasets

Documentation (SCALE_UP_GUIDE.md):
- Quick-start commands
- Resource estimates
- Architecture overview
- Integration path with dimensions schema
- Monitoring and troubleshooting guide
- Cron/Cloud deployment options

Current state: 336 personas (samples)
Target: 1M+ personas (full datasets)

Benefits:
- Complete coverage of major persona datasets

Related: Persona curation, dataset integration, large-scale data processing

source_commit_subject: Add comprehensive dimension constraint matrix (115 rules)
source_commit_body:
Creates realistic persona filtering rules across 1,339 dimensions and 6,644 unique values:
- Age-based constraints: teens can't be married/widowed, age-experience alignment
- Education constraints: low education incompatible with tech domains/executive roles
- Seniority constraints: experience, skill, and career progression alignment
- Domain expertise: domain-specific education and familiarity requirements
- Socioeconomic alignment: education, seniority, and income consistency
- Personality: psychologically rare Big Five combinations
- Language & proficiency: native speaker and multilingualism consistency
- Industry & skills: domain expertise with field familiarity

Files generated:
- dimension_constraints.json: machine-readable sparse format (115 rules)
- dimension_constraints_incompatibilities.csv: index-based matrix lookup
- dimension_constraints_readable.txt: human-readable rule list

Constraint matrix sparsity: 99.9997% (115 rules out of 44.1M possible combinations)

source_commit_subject: Draft: add recommendation chatbot evaluation application (#41)
source_commit_body:
* feat(recommendation-chatbot-eval): RecBot Studio

A workbench for testing and evaluating a conversational recommender — RecAI's
in-process InteRecAgent over a native SASRec ranker — with two surfaces:

- Chat: turn-by-turn debugging; per-turn tool-plan / ranked-candidate inspector.
- Persona Eval: an LLM user-simulator drives a full multi-turn conversation
  against the agent across a 336-persona catalog, then scores the result.

A FastAPI backend serves a built React/Vite SPA at a single origin. Item catalogs
ship as small parquet (data/catalogs/) and the persona catalog is bundled in-app
(data/personas/), so a fresh clone can browse and drive the UI with no large
download; scripts/setup_resources.py fetches the ~1.2 GB recommender bundle for
live recommendations. RecAI is a pristine submodule; its tool-plan parser is made
robust to double-encoded plans via a runtime patch in our bridge.


* studio: make REAL-mode launch cluster-independent (single .env.local)

Remove the author's hardcoded /mira cache paths and absolute venv path from the
committed launch scripts so teammates on other clusters run the app unchanged.
Machine-specific config now lives only in a gitignored .env.local at one
canonical location: the app dir, next to run_demo.sh.

- run_real.sh: drop the /mira HF_HOME / TRANSFORMERS_CACHE /
  SENTENCE_TRANSFORMERS_HOME defaults (caches fall back to ~/.cache).
- requirements.txt: repo-root-relative .venv/bin/python in the install hint.
- run_dev.sh: load .env.local only from the app dir (next to run_demo.sh).
- Add .env.local.example template (OPENAI_API_KEY + optional cache redirect).
- README: point the OpenAI-key step at .env.local.example.

---------

Co-authored-by: Qianfeng Wen <qianfeng.wen@mail.utoronto.ca>

source_commit_subject: ui(polish): fix overlay positioning bug + add a motion vocabulary
source_commit_body:
Root cause of the reported 'clicking Ready pushes the status block off-screen':
the .panel utility (plain CSS after @tailwind utilities) set position:relative
and overrode Tailwind's .absolute by source order, so every 'panel absolute'
popover dropped into flow. Move .panel into @layer components so positioning
utilities win. Affected the preflight popover, the environment popover, etc.

- Popovers/dropdowns: drop the corner-bracket panel treatment, add viewport-safe
  width (max-w calc) + max-h scroll, and a pop-in entrance.
- Drawers + modal: slide-in-right / pop-in panels with a fade-in scrim.
- New reduced-motion-safe motion utilities in index.css: pop-in, slide-in-right,
  rise-in, fade-in (150-220ms, ease-out).

Also adds PRODUCT.md + DESIGN.md for the frontend (impeccable context).
Verified live: Ready popover on-screen, knob dropdown un-clipped, cockpit
reflows cleanly at 800px. tsc + vite build pass.

source_commit_subject: ui(polish): interaction states, motion, contrast & overflow hardening
source_commit_body:
Comprehensive polish sweep across all surfaces (additive — no layout/flow/data
changes; tsc + vite build green):

- Interaction states: hover + active:scale press feedback + disabled states on
  buttons/cards/rows/tabs/knobs; restored a missing focus ring on the clickable
  RecAI bubble; chevrons rotate on open.
- Motion: rise-in (with capped per-item stagger) on content that arrives —
  transcripts, scorecards, answer/trace lists, results; standardized spinners
  (animate-rb-spin) and loading skeletons (animate-rb-pulse on surface-high).
- Contrast (WCAG AA): moved real content off text-text-dim → text-variant/main
  (card/knob descriptions, helper copy, inactive tabs, pipeline labels, footer);
  text-dim reserved for tiny captions/IDs.
- Overflow/off-screen: min-w-0 + truncate/line-clamp/break-words across titles,
  names, messages, footer context; viewport caps on menus; equal-height cards.

Verified live (dark + 760px): no overflow/overlap/off-screen; popover, knob
dropdown, and reflow all correct.
